result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Create bitbucket source configuration.

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll config bitbucket create [address] [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    [address]    Base URL address to bitbucket api

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help        Prints help information                         
    -n, --name        Name of repository source                       
    -t, --type        Type of bitbucket (Cloud and Server (onpremise))
    -u, --username    User name used for basic authentication         
    -p, --password    Pasword used for basic authentication           
        --token       Token used for bearer authentication

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
List dependencies within remote repository.

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll list repo [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help          Prints help information                                 
    -n, --name          Name of repository source that should be used. If not   
                        given, current context will be used                     
    -r, --repository    Name of repository that we should update dependencies in
    -p, --project       Name of a project within which we should update         
                        repositorty. Value is required for Server Bitbucket     
                        source type                                             
    -w, --workspace     Name of a workspace within which we should update       
                        repositorty. Value is required for Cloud Bitbucket      
                        source type

commands.payload:
USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll migrate [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll migrate local -u "package.name:[1.1.0,2.0.0)"

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    local    Migrate dependencies within local project    
    repo     Migrate dependencies within remote repository

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Migrate dependencies within local project

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll migrate local [path] [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll migrate local -u "package.name:[1.1.0,2.0.0)"

ARGUMENTS:
    [path]    Path to solution/csproj which dependency should be updated

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help           Prints help information                                
    -p, --update-path    Path to migration configuration file. It should contain
                         instructions about dependencies changes, in format:    
                             {                                                  
                                 "update": {                                    
                                     "package.name.with.exact.version": "[      
                         1.2.1]",                                               
                                     "package.name.with.range": "(1.0,)"        
                                 }                                              
                             }                                                  
                                                                                
                         Version should be passed in range format:              
                             1.0           --> 1.0 ≤ x                          
                             (,1.0]        --> x ≤ 1.0                          
                             (,1.0)        --> x < 1.0                          
                             [1.0]         --> x == 1.0                         
                             (1.0,)        --> 1.0 < x                          
                             (1.0, 2.0)    --> 1.0 < x < 2.0                    
                             [1.0, 2.0]    --> 1.0 ≤ x ≤ 2.0                    
    -u, --update         Update instractions for migration. Multiple of them can
                         be passed in format:                                   
                         "package.name:[1.2.0,2.0.0)".                          
                         Version should be passed in range format as explained  
                         in update-path parameter                               
        --dry-run        Command will determine dependencies to be updated      
                         without actually updating them

commands.payload:
DESCRIPTION:
Migrate dependencies within local project

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll migrate local [path] [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll migrate local -u "package.name:[1.1.0,2.0.0)"

ARGUMENTS:
    [path]    Path to solution/csproj which dependency should be updated

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help           Prints help information                                
    -p, --update-path    Path to migration configuration file. It should contain
                         instructions about dependencies changes, in format:    
                             {                                                  
                                 "update": {                                    
                                     "package.name.with.exact.version": "[      
                         1.2.1]",                                               
                                     "package.name.with.range": "(1.0,)"        
                                 }                                              
                             }                                                  
                                                                                
                         Version should be passed in range format:              
                             1.0           --> 1.0 ≤ x                          
                             (,1.0]        --> x ≤ 1.0                          
                             (,1.0)        --> x < 1.0                          
                             [1.0]         --> x == 1.0                         
                             (1.0,)        --> 1.0 < x                          
                             (1.0, 2.0)    --> 1.0 < x < 2.0                    
                             [1.0, 2.0]    --> 1.0 ≤ x ≤ 2.0                    
    -u, --update         Update instractions for migration. Multiple of them can
                         be passed in format:                                   
                         "package.name:[1.2.0,2.0.0)".                          
                         Version should be passed in range format as explained  
                         in update-path parameter                               
        --dry-run        Command will determine dependencies to be updated      
                         without actually updating them

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Migrate dependencies within remote repository

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll migrate repo [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help             Prints help information                              
    -n, --name             Name of repository source that should be used. If not
                           given, current context will be used                  
    -r, --repository       Name of repository that we should update dependencies
                           in                                                   
    -p, --project          Name of a project within which we should update      
                           repositorty. Value is required for Server Bitbucket  
                           source type                                          
    -w, --workspace        Name of a workspace within which we should update    
                           repositorty. Value is required for Cloud Bitbucket   
                           source type                                          
    -b, --branch           Name of a branch on which dependencies updates should
                           be commited at                                       
        --commitMessage    Commit message with which dependencies update commit 
                           will going to be done                                
        --update-path      Path to migration configuration file. It should      
                           contain instructions about dependencies changes, in  
                           format:                                              
                               {                                                
                                   "update": {                                  
                                       "package.name.with.exact.version": "[    
                           1.2.1]",                                             
                                       "package.name.with.range": "(1.0,)"      
                                   }                                            
                               }                                                
                                                                                
                           Version should be passed in range format:            
                               1.0           --> 1.0 ≤ x                        
                               (,1.0]        --> x ≤ 1.0                        
                               (,1.0)        --> x < 1.0                        
                               [1.0]         --> x == 1.0                       
                               (1.0,)        --> 1.0 < x                        
                               (1.0, 2.0)    --> 1.0 < x < 2.0                  
                               [1.0, 2.0]    --> 1.0 ≤ x ≤ 2.0                  
    -u, --update           Update instractions for migration. Multiple of them  
                           can be passed in format:                             
                           "package.name:[1.2.0,2.0.0)".                        
                           Version should be passed in range format as explained
                           in update-path parameter                             
        --dry-run          Command will determine dependencies to be updated    
                           without actually updating them

commands.payload:
DESCRIPTION:
Migrate dependencies within remote repository

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll migrate repo [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help             Prints help information                              
    -n, --name             Name of repository source that should be used. If not
                           given, current context will be used                  
    -r, --repository       Name of repository that we should update dependencies
                           in                                                   
    -p, --project          Name of a project within which we should update      
                           repositorty. Value is required for Server Bitbucket  
                           source type                                          
    -w, --workspace        Name of a workspace within which we should update    
                           repositorty. Value is required for Cloud Bitbucket   
                           source type                                          
    -b, --branch           Name of a branch on which dependencies updates should
                           be commited at                                       
        --commitMessage    Commit message with which dependencies update commit 
                           will going to be done                                
        --update-path      Path to migration configuration file. It should      
                           contain instructions about dependencies changes, in  
                           format:                                              
                               {                                                
                                   "update": {                                  
                                       "package.name.with.exact.version": "[    
                           1.2.1]",                                             
                                       "package.name.with.range": "(1.0,)"      
                                   }                                            
                               }                                                
                                                                                
                           Version should be passed in range format:            
                               1.0           --> 1.0 ≤ x                        
                               (,1.0]        --> x ≤ 1.0                        
                               (,1.0)        --> x < 1.0                        
                               [1.0]         --> x == 1.0                       
                               (1.0,)        --> 1.0 < x                        
                               (1.0, 2.0)    --> 1.0 < x < 2.0                  
                               [1.0, 2.0]    --> 1.0 ≤ x ≤ 2.0                  
    -u, --update           Update instractions for migration. Multiple of them  
                           can be passed in format:                             
                           "package.name:[1.2.0,2.0.0)".                        
                           Version should be passed in range format as explained
                           in update-path parameter                             
        --dry-run          Command will determine dependencies to be updated    
                           without actually updating them

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Update dependencies within local project.

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll update local [path] [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    [path]    Path to solution/csproj which dependency should be updated

OPTIONS:
                          DEFAULT                                               
    -h, --help                       Prints help information                    
        --dry-run                    Command will determine dependencies to be  
                                     updated without actually updating them     
        --prerelease                 Command will look for pre-release versions 
                                     of packages                                
    -v, --version-lock    None       Specifies whether the package should be    
                                     locked to the current Major or Minor       
                                     version. Possible values: None (default),  
                                     Major, Minor

commands.payload:
DESCRIPTION:
Update dependencies within local project.

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll update local [path] [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:

OPTIONS:

    -h, --help                       Prints help information                    
        --dry-run                    Command will determine dependencies to be  
                                     updated without actually updating them     
        --prerelease                 Command will look for pre-release versions 
                                     of packages                                
    -v, --version-lock    None       Specifies whether the package should be    
                                     locked to the current Major or Minor       
                                     version. Possible values: None (default),  
                                     Major, Minor

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Update dependencies within remote repository.

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll update repo [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
                           DEFAULT                                              
    -h, --help                        Prints help information                   
    -n, --name                        Name of repository source that should be  
                                      used. If not given, current context will  
                                      be used                                   
    -r, --repository                  Name of repository that we should update  
                                      dependencies in                           
    -p, --project                     Name of a project within which we should  
                                      update repositorty. Value is required for 
                                      Server Bitbucket source type              
    -w, --workspace                   Name of a workspace within which we should
                                      update repositorty. Value is required for 
                                      Cloud Bitbucket source type               
    -b, --branch                      Name of a branch on which dependencies    
                                      updates should be commited at             
        --commitMessage               Commit message with which dependencies    
                                      update commit will going to be done       
        --prerelease                  Command will look for pre-release versions
                                      of packages                               
    -v, --version-lock     None       Specifies whether the package should be   
                                      locked to the current Major or Minor      
                                      version. Possible values: None (default), 
                                      Major, Minor                              
        --dry-run                     Command will determine dependencies to be 
                                      updated without actually updating them

commands.payload:
DESCRIPTION:
Update dependencies within remote repository.

USAGE:
    sharp-dependency.cli.dll update repo [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
                           DEFAULT                                              
    -h, --help                        Prints help information                   
    -n, --name                        Name of repository source that should be  
                                      used. If not given, current context will  
                                      be used                                   
    -r, --repository                  Name of repository that we should update  
                                      dependencies in                           
    -p, --project                     Name of a project within which we should  
                                      update repositorty. Value is required for 
                                      Server Bitbucket source type              
    -w, --workspace                   Name of a workspace within which we should
                                      update repositorty. Value is required for 
                                      Cloud Bitbucket source type               
    -b, --branch                      Name of a branch on which dependencies    
                                      updates should be commited at             
        --commitMessage               Commit message with which dependencies    
                                      update commit will going to be done       
        --prerelease                  Command will look for pre-release versions
                                      of packages                               
    -v, --version-lock     None       Specifies whether the package should be   
                                      locked to the current Major or Minor      
                                      version. Possible values: None (default), 
                                      Major, Minor                              
        --dry-run                     Command will determine dependencies to be 
                                      updated without actually updating them
